frames: The agent begins to lift its body from a low, horizontal position, extending its rear limbs.
It continues to rise, shifting its weight onto its rear limbs while its front limbs bend upwards.
The agent achieves an upright, almost standing posture, then rapidly loses balance and begins to fall forward.
It collapses completely, landing flat on its stomach with all limbs making contact with the ground.
The agent lies prone, with its body horizontal and its limbs slightly bent and splayed.
It attempts a slight push-up motion, causing its body to rock gently up and down.
The rocking motion persists, with the agent's torso lifting slightly off the ground.
The agent settles back into a stable, prone position, exhibiting no further significant movement.
